# img_resize

- purpose: Resize and compress images (PNG/JPG). Pure Rust, no network dependencies.
- version: 0.2.0
- language: Rust

## Command Syntax

```
img_resize [--json] r_resize <PATH> [-m <SIZE>] [--rw <WIDTH> --rh <HEIGHT>] [-c <FILE>] [-j]
```

## Arguments

- `<PATH>`: Image file or directory path (PNG/JPG)
- `-m <SIZE>`: Max pixel value for proportional scaling (preserves aspect ratio)
- `--rw <WIDTH>` + `--rh <HEIGHT>`: Exact resize dimensions (must use together)
- `-c <FILE>`: YAML config file for batch multi-size output
- `-j`: Force convert output to JPG
- `--json`: Structured JSON output

## Resize Modes (mutually exclusive)

1. Proportional: `-m 800` — scale so max dimension <= 800px
2. Exact: `--rw 1920 --rh 1080` — resize to exact dimensions
3. Config file: `-c config.yaml` — generate multiple sizes per YAML spec

## YAML Config Format

```yaml
vec_size:
  - [1920, 1080]
  - [800, 600]
vec_f:
  - "output/large.png"
  - "output/small.png"
base_f: "/output/base/path"
```

## Examples

```bash
img_resize r_resize -m 800 image.jpg
img_resize r_resize --rw 1920 --rh 1080 image.jpg
img_resize r_resize -m 1024 images/
img_resize --json r_resize -m 800 image.jpg
```

## Notes

- `tinyfy` subcommand is disabled (OpenSSL/musl incompatibility)
- Supports batch directory processing
- Exit code 1 on error, JSON error envelope when `--json` is set

## Install

```bash
cargo build --release
```
